set.body: Close the measured performance gap vs pure-Java (F2J/dev.ludovic.netlib fallback) Breeze on core BLAS ops. Smoke evidence 2026-07-10: Breeze ahead ~2.9x on dot (n=65536) and ~2.4x on gemm (n=64); forked sweep results in session scratchpad breeze-sweep.json gate the exact work list. Known untuned spots (flagged during remediation): gemm block size=64 and the 64^3 blocking threshold; dot/axpy/norm loop structure (F2J uses 4-8x manual unrolling — likely the dot gap); gemv row-vs-column traversal. Approach: read F2J/netlib-java reference loop shapes (vendor/breeze checkout may help locate them), tune gale's DoubleKernels with unroll-and-jam + blocking sweeps measured via the paired JMH harness (sbt 'benchmarksJVM/Jmh/run .*Breeze.*'), keep kernels pure shared Scala (JS must not regress — re-run benchSmokeJS), and verify bit-identical results are NOT required (kernels may reassociate) BUT parityTest and testAll must stay green and cross-platform determinism of gale-vs-gale must hold (same input -> same output on JVM and JS individually; reassociation changing results vs previous gale versions is acceptable if tests/laws still pass). Acceptance: gale >= pure-JVM Breeze on a majority of the 14 benchmark pairs at the largest measured size, no correctness regressions. Native-BLAS Breeze remains out of scope (v0.5).
